name: MoveSpaces
description: Move the focused window to the macOS Space immediately to the left or right of the current Space, on the same screen.
author: Cato Kolås <cato.kolas@gmail.com>

config.label: Follow window
config.description: If on (default), the viewer follows the window to its new Space — like moving a window between workspaces on Linux. Off snaps the view back after the move.

config.label: Wrap at edges
config.description: If on, moving past the leftmost/rightmost Space wraps around to the opposite end. Off (default) is a silent no-op at edges.

config.label: Skip fullscreen Spaces
config.description: If on (default), fullscreen-app Spaces are stepped over when looking for the destination Space.

optionalModules.name: hs._ckol.movetospace
optionalModules.description: Moves windows to a specific Space via SkyLight private APIs. Bypasses hs.spaces.moveWindowToSpace, which silently no-ops on macOS 26+ for most windows. Without it the Spoon falls back to a Ctrl+arrow drag simulation.

knownIssues.title: Limited on macOS 26+
knownIssues.description: Apple's WindowServer in macOS 26 (Sonoma's successor track) gates cross-process Space moves at the system level. hs.spaces.moveWindowToSpace silently no-ops for most windows, and the Ctrl+arrow drag fallback also stops working reliably. Install the optional hs._ckol.movetospace native helper for the best chance of moving windows — though on macOS 26.5 even the helper only moves Hammerspoon's own windows.

knownIssues.title: Ctrl+arrow fallback may collide with system shortcuts
knownIssues.description: Without the hs._ckol.movetospace native helper, the Spoon simulates the system Ctrl+left / Ctrl+right Space-switch shortcuts to move the window. If you've remapped those in System Settings → Keyboard → Shortcuts → Mission Control, the fallback will misbehave. The native helper bypasses this entirely.
